title.en: Cleaning for Pesach: The Complete Chametz Removal Guide
title.zh: 为逾越节清洁:完整的发酵食物清除指南
title.he: ניקיון לפסח: המדריך המלא להסרת חמץ

description: Room-by-room guide to searching for and removing chametz before Passover, including bedikat chametz and selling chametz.
author: Torah Light Editorial Team
header_image_description: A serene Chinese brushstroke (水墨) landscape depicting a traditional home at twilight. In the foreground, soft candlelight glows from a window where a figure searches carefully with a feather and wooden spoon. The scene transitions from darkness (representing chametz/slavery) to the soft golden light of dawn (representing freedom and purity). Mountains rise in the misty background under a crescent moon. Color palette: deep indigos and blacks transitioning to warm golds and soft grays. Style: Shan shui (山水) painting with delicate ink washes, embodying the contemplative and meticulous nature of the Pesach cleaning ritual. Mood: meditative, purposeful, hopeful.
target_audience: Chinese native Mandarin speakers with no prior Jewish knowledge
cultural_parallels: Spring cleaning before Lunar New Year (扫尘)
Removing old to make room for new blessings
